items.title: Agentjacking: New Attack Hijacks AI Coding Agents via Sentry MCP Injection
items.summary: Tenet Security disclosed a novel attack class that injects malicious commands into Sentry error reports, which AI coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex) execute as trusted guidance on developer machines. AWS keys, GitHub tokens, and git credentials are silently exfiltrated. 85% success rate in controlled tests across 100+ targets including a Fortune 500 firm. Sentry declined to fix at the root-cause level.
items.why: Any developer using AI coding agents alongside Sentry is exposed with no platform-level patch. Attacker needs only a project DSN to inject commands; no authentication required.

items.title: CVE-2026-11645: Chrome V8 Zero-Day (CVSS 8.8) Actively Exploited In the Wild — Patch Now
items.summary: Google patched CVE-2026-11645 on June 9, 2026 — an out-of-bounds memory access in V8 (Chrome's JavaScript engine) enabling remote code execution inside the sandbox via a crafted HTML page. No file download required; simply visiting a malicious page is sufficient. This is the fifth Chrome zero-day exploited in the wild in 2026. Fixed in Chrome 149.0.7827.102/103.
items.why: Drive-by exploitation via malicious webpage; all Chrome users on prior builds remain vulnerable. The fifth in-the-wild V8 zero-day in 2026 signals sustained, active targeting of the Chrome engine.

items.title: CVE-2026-50751: Check Point VPN Auth Bypass (CVSS 9.3) Weaponized by Qilin Ransomware Affiliate
items.summary: A critical authentication bypass (CVSS 9.3) in Check Point VPN's deprecated IKEv1 implementation lets unauthenticated attackers establish full VPN sessions without credentials. A Qilin ransomware affiliate has exploited it since May 7, 2026. CISA added it to the KEV catalog on June 8 and gave federal agencies 3 days to patch.
items.why: Actively weaponized for ransomware initial access with a CISA 3-day federal mandate. Any org running legacy IKEv1 VPN configs has had confirmed adversarial exposure for 5+ weeks.

items.title: APT28 Weaponizes CVE-2026-21509 Within 24 Hours, Hides C2 in Cloud Storage filen.io
items.summary: Trellix identified a Russian APT28 espionage campaign targeting European maritime and transport agencies across Poland, Slovenia, Turkey, Greece, UAE, and Ukraine. Attackers weaponized Microsoft Office vuln CVE-2026-21509 within 24 hours of disclosure. The campaign deploys two novel implants — NotDoor (Outlook VBA backdoor) and BeardShell (C++ payload) — using legitimate cloud storage filen.io for C2 to blend into normal traffic.
items.why: Nation-state actor with a 24-hour patch-to-exploit cadence; legitimate cloud-service C2 evades most network detection. New malware families and critical infrastructure targeting make this high-priority for defenders.

items.title: Microsoft June 2026 Patch Tuesday: Record 206 CVEs, 3 Zero-Days, Two CVSS 9.8 RCEs in Windows Kernel and HTTP.sys
items.summary: Microsoft's June 2026 Patch Tuesday patches a record 206 CVEs (32 critical) including 3 actively exploited zero-days. Standouts: CVE-2026-45657 (Windows Kernel use-after-free RCE, CVSS 9.8), CVE-2026-47291 (HTTP.sys unauthenticated RCE, CVSS 9.8), three Hyper-V VM-escape flaws, and 11 Remote Desktop Client RCEs. Analysts attribute the volume spike to AI-assisted vulnerability discovery.
items.why: Two CVSS 9.8 no-interaction RCEs in foundational Windows components (Kernel, HTTP.sys) demand immediate patch prioritization. Hyper-V VM-escape bugs threaten cloud and virtual infrastructure. AI-fueled vuln discovery is pushing patch volumes to new highs.
